content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Legal Division                                    Date : 3/15/76

                                                       [?] 96-03-042

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Nan

Subject: Telephone Conversation - Dave White, 714/833-8312


Here's another "are we a committee" question for your
consideration:

The group is affiliated with a political party.
It hosts receptions at which all candidates for
a particular office in the party are presented.
The group provides refreshments and a no-host cock-
tail bar. The admission fee is $2 - 5 and is used to
cover the costs of the refreshments, hall rental,
publicity of event, etc. The group never endorses
candidates nor makes contributions to or expenditures
in behalf of candidates or measures.

Can the group become a committee by virtue of collect-
ing $500 or more in a year in admission fees or by
making expenditures of $500 or more to provide forums
for candidates?


Nan gave tentative advice that
the ag. is not a committee

(3)

Gardeners Association, Inc.

ROBERT IDA
10772 Penn St., P.O. Box 85
Garden Grove, CA 92642                                714-537-5293

March 22, 1976

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 207
Suite 222, 925 J. Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE:     Subject: Proper Political Fund Raising Practices.

Dear Sir:

Being a past-president of the Orange County Gardeners' Association, Inc. Our Organization is incorporated with the State of California as a non-profit body. As a leader of organization, I have constantly called upon to donated money from fund raisings for political candidates. I usually donate on my own, individually; however, as our organization membership grew in numbers ( over 700 members ), my commitment to the political candidates increased. Now, Orange County, now has the second largest population in the State of California.

My organization want to relieve me of some of my financial burden I am bearing by paying whole or in part. I am resisting this offer because I do not know if it's proper to even consider their suggestion. I know for the last twentys the candidates do accept donations and sent you a receipt. What I do not know is that could my nation be reimbursed by my organization? Could any of these contributions be made by my organization? I know that any political system must have adequate fund to function and I would like to know where and how I can get information by purchasing of by other methods of concerning proper fair political practices, I do hope that you will find it possible assist me on this matter. Thanking you on an anticipated cooperation.

Yours truly,

Robert S. Ida
Robert S. Ida
Past-president, Orange County Gardeners Association, Inc.

embedding.qa_text: This memorandum is from the Fair Political Practices Commission to the Legal Division, dated 3/15/76. The subject of the memo is a telephone conversation with Dave White at 714/833-8312. The content of the memo states: Here's another "are we a committee" question for your consideration: The group is affiliated with a political party. It hosts receptions at which all candidates for a particular office in the party are presented. The group provides refreshments and a no-host cocktail bar. The admission fee is $2 - 5 and is used to cover the costs of the refreshments, hall rental, publicity of event, etc. The group never endorses candidates nor makes contributions to or expenditures in behalf of candidates or measures. Can the group become a committee by virtue of collecting $500 or more in a year in admission fees or by making expenditures of $500 or more to provide forums for candidates? Nan gave tentative advice that this org. is not a committee. March 22, 1976 Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman c/o Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Suite 522, 925 J. Street Sacramento, California 95814 RE: Subject: Proper Political Fund Raising Practices. Dear Sir: Being a past-president of the Orange County Gardeners' Association, Inc. Our Organization is incorporated with the State of California as a non-profit body. As a leader of an organization, I am constantly called upon to donated money to fund raisings for political candidates. I usually donate on my own, individually; however, as our organization membership grow in numbers (over 300 members), my commitments to the political candidates increase in numbers, too. As you know, Orange County, now has the second largest population in the State of California. My organization want to relieve me of some of my financial burden I am bearing by paying whole or in part. I am resisting this offer because I do not know if it is proper to even consider their suggestions. I know for the last twenties the candidates do accept donations and sent you a receipts. What I do not know is that could my donation be reimbursed by my organization? Could any of these contributions be made by my organization? I know that any political system must have adequate fund to function and I would like to know where and how I can get information by purchasing or by other methods of concerning proper fair political practices. I do hope that you will find it possible assist me on this matter. Thanking you on an anticipated cooperation. Yours truly, Robert S. Ida Past-President, Orange County Gardeners' Association, Inc.
